AgentKey

AgentKey

Maker

Hey PH , I'm Cong, Founding engineer at Chainbase here. I've spent the past few months building AgentKey, a one-stop live data marketplace for your agent, and I want to share the three problems that took us the longest to get right.

1. Tool discovery [...] You wire up one API for search, another for scraping, another for social, another for market data. You babysit a pile of keys and bills, and you basically have to understand the plumbing before you can even start. Most people who'd benefit from this never get past that part. So we built AgentKey. You install it like a plugin, and your agent gets access to a whole marketplace of capabilities behind a single account. No juggling APIs, no keys to manage, no need to be technical. One account, one bill, and your agent suddenly gets a lot more powerful. Right now the marketplace is organized into a few tiers, and it keeps growing: - Everyday tools: search, web scraping, social media - Professional data: finance, crypto, business data - Life and travel: weather, maps, trip planning, and more It

results.title: Top Data Providers for AI Agents: Real-Time APIs, MCP, and AI-Ready Data
results.content:
Choose Bright Data if your AI agent needs live web access, scraping, extraction, navigation, or web-scale research. It is especially useful for agents that need to retrieve information from the open web rather than rely only on predefined B2B datasets.

Choose Crustdata if your AI agent needs real-time company and people signals for sales, recruiting, market monitoring, or investment research. It is a strong option when the workflow depends on timely business changes and signal detection.

Choose Explorium if your AI agent is focused on GTM enrichment, business entity intelligence, account intelligence, or sales and marketing workflows. It is especially relevant for teams that need enrichment data connected to revenue operations and go-to-market execution. [...] Choose Scrapin.io if your AI agent mainly needs real-time professional profile and company enrichment. It can be useful for focused lead enrichment, company enrichment, and recruiting workflows, though teams building broader AI agent infrastructure should validate whether it covers all their requirements before committing.

### How to Choose an Employee Data Provider for Fresh, Real-Time B2B Data in 2026 [...] Traditional datasets still have value, particularly for model training, historical analysis, market research, benchmarking, and offline enrichment. But production AI agents typically need more than a one-time data export. They need data infrastructure that supports retrieval, reasoning, monitoring, enrichment, and workflow automation in real time or near real time.

The difference is not only about format. It is about how easily an AI system can access the data, understand it, filter it, combine it with other context, and use it to trigger the next step. A static dataset may be useful for analysis, but a data provider for AI agents should support more dynamic access methods that fit into live workflows.

### Quandl

Quandl is an online platform for financial and alternative data marketplaces and platforms, offering access to millions of insurance policies in the United States for specialty vehicles such as motorcycles, motorhomes, travel trailers, recreational marine, and off-road vehicles like ATVs, golf carts, and snowmobiles. It also provides data monetization and analysis tools, API access, and resources to help users better understand the data available.

### Eagle Alpha

Eagle Alpha is a leading platform in the alternative data space, connecting buyers and sellers of unique datasets to unlock actionable insights. With a network of over 1,000 datasets covering various industries, including retail, technology, and healthcare, Eagle Alpha empowers financial institutions and corporations to gain a competitive edge. Their platform features advanced analytics tools and data visualization capabilities, enabling users to extract valuable signals and make informed investment decisions.
